one_time_description: Columbia River Treaty revenue sharing - non-recurring provincial transfer of $9,255,719 received in 2024 (nil in 2023); also Rights and Titles expenses of $8,458,860 include land settlement distribution of $1,355,928 and own source revenue distribution of $6,995,913
economic_notes: akisq'nuk is one of four Ktunaxa Nation communities. Holds investments in multiple Ktunaxa Nation entities including KHLP (20% LP interest), SHL (20% interest), SDC, SEMHL, BCFN Gaming LP. New partnerships formed in 2023: AFN Holdings LP and AFN Enterprises LP. Elkhorn Ranch Settlement trust established September 26, 2023 with balance of $4,156,721. Columbia River Treaty revenue sharing of $9,255,719 received for first time. Impact Management and Benefits Agreement ongoing. Campground operations active.
notes: Significant one-time items in 2024: Columbia River Treaty revenue sharing $9,255,719 (nil in prior year) and own source revenue distribution expense of $6,995,913 recorded under Rights and Titles. Prior year (2023) had massive Rights and Titles expenses of $22,240,326 including land settlement distribution of $22,148,600 related to Elkhorn Ranch Settlement. Three consolidated subsidiaries: Eva Joseph Early Learning & Cultural Society, Akisq'nuk Housing Society, 1285651 BC Ltd. Three GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: akisq'nuk Natural Resources LP, AFN Holdings LP, AFN Enterprises LP. Nation is not compliant with First Nations Financial Transparency Act and First Nations Fiscal Management Act filing deadlines. Tangible capital assets net book value taken from Schedule 1 as $17,274,335 (2024 column). Auditor notes prior year statements were audited by a different auditor. Elkhorn Ranch Settlement PCD Minors Trust of $4,156,721 excluded from financial statements.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
